course_metadata.location: (All in person sections – Zach 344) and section 599 – ONLINE

chunks.title: Office Hours (Appointment Policy)
chunks.content: Outside of my office hours, you MUST set up an appointment. If you would like to set up a meeting, please email me 24 hours before the meeting time and provide a few times that might work with your schedule. I will try to find a time that works for both. You should prepare questions before we meet.

chunks.title: Course Description
chunks.content:
Principles of economic equivalence; time value of money; analysis of single and multiple investments;
comparison of alternatives; capital recovery and after-tax analysis of economic projects

chunks.title: Course Prerequisites
chunks.content: MATH 152 or MATH 172 and ability to use Microsoft Excel.

chunks.title: Course Learning Outcome
chunks.content:
• Make engineering decisions based on an economic analysis of alternatives
• Explain and demonstrate the principles of economic equivalence and time value of money
• Formulate and solve equations using interest factors and formulas
• Distinguish between nominal and effective interest rates and convert between them
• Identify and determine constituent cash flows of single and/or multiple investments
• Examine, diagram, and compare investment alternatives on an economic basis using appropriate
terminology
• Conclude and justify the best investment alternative(s) based on appropriate analysis
• Develop and construct spreadsheet models to investigate alternatives
• Determine and utilize appropriate capital recovery methods for alternatives

chunks.title: Textbook and/or Resource Materials
chunks.content:
Engineering Economic Analysis (14 th Edition)
D. Newnan, T. G. Eschenbach, J. P. Lavelle, and N. Lewis (February 19, 2019)
ISBN: 978-0-19-093191-9
Oxford University Press

We have been notified that this textbook is no longer available in print version. However, the edition is
available with a new (ISBN: 9780190063467) as it is now included as a pack and contains an access code.
The option I recommend is to rent the digital version of the 14th edition, at the bookstore or anywhere you
can find it at a lesser price. I am not aware of what the bookstore charges for the book, but I am adding a
link below where you may rent it for $49.99 if that price is better:

https://www.vitalsource.com/products/engineering-economic-analysis-don-newnan-ted-eschenbach-
v9780190931940?term=9780190063467

chunks.title: Grading Policy
chunks.content:
Homework assignments are due on the due date posted on Canvas. Please submit all work through
Canvas. You should submit your work on time. Late submissions are penalized 10% per each late day
and are only accepted on the week the work is due, after that, no late work will be accepted. Please
note that homework assignments have different value and weight, so please do not assume they are all
equal. In addition, there might be in-class assignments / checking for understanding, short quizzes, in-
class quizzes, three tests during the semester, and one special assignment worth a test grade. No make-up
examinations will be allowed unless prior arrangements have been made and only if you have a university
accepted excuse. Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not considered
“late” and is exempted from the late work policy (Student Rule 7), but this HAS to be pre-arranged with
the professor.

The components of the course grade and their weights are as follows: Assignments, 20%; Quizzes, 20%;
Tests, 60%. The university letter grade policy is followed (>=90%: A; 80-89%: B; 70 – 79%: C; 60 –
69%: D; below 60%: F.)

There will be no curve at the end of this semester, so please make sure you submit your work on time!

chunks.title: Other Course Information Items (Course Website)
chunks.content:
The course website will be maintained through http://canvas.tamu.edu/. This site will contain
announcements and other information concerning the course. In addition, it will be used to distribute class
materials (homework assignment, notes,) and grades. Check the website regularly, I will send all
information and communications through Canvas through the “Announcements” section. Any information
posted there will be as valid as if it was mentioned in class.

chunks.title: Other Course Information Items (Academic Integrity)
chunks.content:
It is expected that all work submitted in this class will be done individually. If you got any help with
homework, please cite that person in your write-up. For example, “I thank John Smith for helping me
solve this problem.” If you worked together with one or more other people, then each of you should cite
the other(s).

chunks.title: FE Exam / Calculator Policy
chunks.content:
A major reason for taking this class is to prepare all engineering majors for the Fundamentals of
Engineering (FE) exam, as well as to prepare Industrial Engineering majors for other ISEN courses. To
this end, students are strongly encouraged to use calculators that are approved by NCEES for use on the
FE. The NCEES website (http://ncees.org/exams/calculator-policy/) provides a list of the current
approved calculator brands and models.

chunks.title: Exam Administration and Accommodations
chunks.content:
Most exams are administered through Canvas using Respondus proctoring software. They will be
closed-book/notes/smartphone. Calculator is allowed if needed. If you have requested special
accommodations, you might not need to schedule your test through the testing center (More
information will be provided after the semester begins. Please come see me if you have questions.

chunks.title: Attendance Policy
chunks.content:
The university views class attendance and participation as an individual student responsibility. Students
are expected to attend class and to complete all assignments.

Please refer to Student Rule 7 in its entirety for information about excused absences, including
definitions, and related documentation and timelines.

chunks.title: Makeup Work Policy
chunks.content:
Students will be excused from attending class on the day of a graded activity or when attendance
contributes to a student's grade, for the reasons stated in Student Rule 7, or other reason deemed
appropriate by the instructor.

Please refer to Student Rule 7 in its entirety for information about makeup work, including definitions,
and related documentation and timelines.

Absences related to Title IX of the Education Amendments of 1972 may necessitate a period of more than
30 days for make-up work, and the timeframe for make-up work should be agreed upon by the student
and instructor" (Student Rule 7, Section 7.4.1).

"The instructor is under no obligation to provide an opportunity for the student to make up work missed
because of an unexcused absence" (Student Rule 7, Section 7.4.2).

Students who request an excused absence are expected to uphold the Aggie Honor Code and Student
Conduct Code. (See Student Rule 24.)

chunks.title: Statement on Mental Health and Wellness
chunks.content:
Texas A&M University recognizes that mental health and wellness are critical factors that influence a
student's academic success and overall wellbeing. Students are encouraged to engage in healthy self-care
by utilizing available resources and services on your campus

Students who need someone to talk to can contact Counseling & Psychological Services (CAPS) or call
the TAMU Helpline (979-845-2700) from 4:00 p.m. to 8:00 a.m. weekdays and 24 hours on weekends. 24-
hour emergency help is also available through the National Suicide Prevention Hotline (800-273-8255)
or at suicidepreventionlifeline.org.
